a: An individual prediction of anticipated cellular network availability.
An enumeration of levels of confidence for a prediction or one of its properties.
A high confidence level in the prediction.
A low confidence level in the prediction.
A medium confidence level in the prediction.
A type that represents the confidence in various aspects of the prediction.
An enumeration of levels of impact for a predicted event.
An impact level for severe interruption, including substantially lowered throughput and the potential to lose data capability.
An impact level for slightly lower throughput and possibly some service interruption.
An impact level for moderately lower throughput and service interruption.
A type that provides discrete time intervals to express the expected duration of a predicted event.
A type that represents errors encountered while using the WirelessInsights framework.
An unexpected error occurred while setting up the event stream.
The device doesn’t currently support service predictions.
A class that provices cellular service predictions about upcoming events and anomalies.
Receive notifications for anticipated changes in cellular data service conditions.
A score that represents the level of confidence in various aspects of the prediction.
Confidence of the duration if the predicted event occurs.
The expected impact of the predicted event.
Creates a service prediction provider.
An interval for an issue expected to last more than five minutes.
An interval for an issue expected to last approximately five minutes.
An interval for an issue expected to last approximately 10 seconds or fewer.
The expected duration of the predicted event.
The start time of the predicted event.
Confidence that the predicted event will occur.
An asychronous sequence of current predictions.
An interval for an issue expected to last approximately one minute or fewer.
Confidence of the start time if the predicted event occurs.
